actionables.other_change: Proactive engagement with supervisory authorities and timely escalation of data risks.
actionables.policy_change: Institutions must embed supervisory reporting adequacy into governance and control frameworks.
actionables.process_change: Enhance pre-submission validation, template activation/deactivation aligned with regulatory perimeter, and escalation protocols.
actionables.reporting_change: Improve accuracy, completeness, timeliness, and traceability of supervisory reporting submissions.
actionables.tech_data_change: Implement validation mechanisms and ensure compatibility with MFSA, ECB, and EBA systems to reduce data quality issues and operational rejections.

other_dates.description: Period when the targeted review of supervisory reporting adequacy for LSIs was conducted
Year when the Compliance Outcomes-Based Supervisory framework was introduced
Year when the Compliance Outcomes-Based Supervisory framework was extended to all financial services sectors
Publication date of the latest version (v5.6) of the EBA Filing Rules
Date of Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2021/451 laying down implementing technical standards for supervisory reporting
Date of Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2021/453 laying down implementing technical standards for specific reporting requirements for market risk
Date of Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2024/3117 superseding previous regulations on supervisory reporting

impact_summary.objective: To assess and ensure the adequacy of supervisory reporting by Less Significant Institutions (LSIs) in Malta, supporting sound prudential judgements and supervisory decisions.
impact_summary.risk_impact: Persistent deficiencies in supervisory reporting can lead to breaches of binding regulations, negatively affecting institutions' risk profiles and potentially leading to supervisory interventions.
impact_summary.what_changed: The MFSA conducted a targeted review of supervisory reporting adequacy for LSIs, embedding supervisory reporting adequacy into its outcomes-based supervisory framework and emphasizing data accuracy, completeness, and timeliness as key indicators of operational resilience and governance.
impact_summary.why_it_matters: Accurate and timely supervisory reporting is critical for effective oversight, risk assessment, and maintaining the integrity of both national and European supervisory frameworks, impacting financial stability and regulatory compliance.
key_requirements: Embed robust data governance frameworks with clear accountability and escalation protocols.
Reinforce pre-submission controls and validation mechanisms to minimize breaches and omissions.
Ensure completeness by aligning reporting templates strictly with regulatory perimeter and technical specifications.
Maintain full traceability in submissions with accurate classification of resubmission reasons and comprehensive explanations.
Engage proactively with the MFSA, escalating data risks promptly and utilizing available technical guidance and resources.

rules: EBA Filing Rules v5.6 published May 2025 (https://www.eba.europa.eu/regulation-and-policy/supervisory-reporting)
Banking Rules BR/16, BR/24, BR/31 issued under the Banking Act (Cap. 371) (https://www.mfsa.mt/legislation/banking-act/)
